source_text: 19. Tabbing a Correspondence Package. Tab the signature page (if not the first page), enclosures, and background material. Label the tabs as appropriate. Figure 7-10 illustrates an assembled correspondence package with tabs. Take care so tabs can be removed without defacing the document.
plain_language_rule: Tab the signature page (unless it is the first page), all enclosures, and all background material in a correspondence package. Label each tab as appropriate. Ensure tabs can be removed without defacing any document.
notes: This is a physical paper-handling instruction for staff assembling correspondence packages. Items requiring tabs: signature page (when it is not page 1), enclosures, and background material. Tab placement is not specified — deferred to Figure 7-10. Tab labeling uses "as appropriate" with no format, numbering scheme, or content standard provided. The "defacing" instruction is a preservation concern: use removable tabs that leave no adhesive residue or surface damage. No digital/electronic equivalent is implied.
